lines.text: Ms. Alvarez taps the board twice before turning around. THE ECOSYSTEM TERRARIUM PROJECT — DUE FRIDAY, underlined twice, MODEL + LIVE Q&A beneath it.
Groups are on the board. You'll build a self-sustaining terrarium, and Friday, you defend it — live, in front of me and each other. No scripts.
lines.action: taps pen twice on the desk

lines.text: Called it. At least it's not the volcano group. So over volcanoes.
lines.action: thumb moving under the desk, half-watching his phone

lines.text: I already made a rough materials list. Just in case. It's probably overkill—
We haven't even picked seats yet.
lines.action: leans back, arms crossed, watching everyone size each other up

lines.text: Okay but— should we figure out who's doing what first? Or...
lines.action: trails off, glancing around the table

lines.text: Relax, new girl. I got a system.
That's what he said before the egg drop.
That was ONE time—
Sorry— sorry, no, that was actually pretty bad.
Four days. Use them well.
Okay, so, don't freak out—
lines.action: slides a nearly-finished terrarium onto the table like a magic trick

lines.text: That's never a good start—
It's my sister's. From last year. Same unit and everything. We tweak the labels, repot one plant so it looks fresh, we're done by Tuesday. Nobody's gonna check.

lines.action: goes very still, watching the player instead of Jordan

lines.text: That's not our research though. If she asks about soil pH, or the— we didn't calculate any of that.
Nobody asks that stuff. She asks like three questions tops. It's not that deep, Maya.
I guess I just... I don't know.
So what are we doing.
lines.action: taps pencil against his knuckles, eyes still on the player

lines.text: C'mon. It's just labels.
lines.action: leans back, easy grin, eyes flicking to the player

lines.text: It's just labels. That's what he keeps saying. Like saying it enough times makes it true.
SEE? Genius squad.
This says Chlorophytum comosum but I think— I think that's not actually the plant in slot three. Should I—
lines.action: relabeling in handwriting that isn't hers, eyes down

lines.text: Maya. Nobody's gonna check.
Right. Okay.
That was fast.
Yeah. Faster than I thought.
Mm.
The terrarium sits on the table, glass gleaming, looking almost too perfect for four kids who did almost nothing.
Okay but— what if we just... actually did it? Split it up for real.
Man, why are you making this harder than it's gotta be?
Because it's not our project if it's not our terrarium.
I can do the research part. I already have three sources bookmarked, actually, I just didn't want to—
lines.action: stops herself, quick relief in her voice

lines.text: I'll build it. Somebody's gotta make sure it doesn't fall apart. Literally.
Fine. FINE. I'll do labels. But when this takes till Thursday night, I'm saying I told you so.
Deal.

It's slower. Messier. Maya double-checks her sources three times. Deshawn measures the glass twice before cutting anything. But by the end of the session, the terrarium between them is actually, entirely theirs.
A friend of Jordan's passes the workroom table, and Jordan can't resist showing off.
Yeah, ecosystem's basically self-sustaining, we barely had to do anyth—
His elbow catches the edge of the table. The terrarium tips, hits the floor. Glass cracks. Dirt and a small fern spill across the tile.
Oh no— oh no oh no—
Okay. Nobody move, there's glass. What happened?
Maya was holding it — she dropped it!
lines.action: blurted, almost before he's thought it through
goes very still, eyes on the player

lines.text: Walk me through it. Slowly.
lines.action: pen tap, calm, looking between them

lines.text: I— that's not— okay, my bad, I panicked, whatever—
Glass breaks. That's not the interesting part.
The interesting part is what everybody said happened next.
You okay?
Yeah. Yeah, I'm okay.
lines.action: exhales like she's been holding her breath an hour

lines.text: Deshawn, get the dustpan from the supply closet. Rest of you, give me a second with this table.
That was you, right? Not just reflex.
lines.action: quiet aside to the player, getting up

lines.text: I don't know. Maybe both.
Jordan doesn't look at anyone the rest of the period.
Ellis finds Maya alone at the end of a lunch table, picking at food she isn't eating.
Hey. Can I sit?
Free country.
Maya, I— I should've said something back there. When Jordan—
You didn't say anything either.
...Yeah. I know.
It's fine. I mean it's not — it's not fine, but. I get it. Nobody wants to be the one who makes it weird.
lines.action: taps pencil in sets of three without noticing

lines.text: That's not— I wasn't trying to protect him. I just froze.
I had it in my hands for like two seconds, I swear, I don't even know what happened, I just froze too. So. We're both frozen people, I guess.
I'm really sorry.
I have a list. Of things I should've said. It's getting really long, Ellis.
Yeah. Mine too, probably.
You could still say something. Before Friday, I mean.
lines.action: finally looks up, ghost of a smile

lines.text: Yeah— yeah, that's, that's what happened.
Maya. You need to be more careful with shared materials. Are we clear?
...Yes, Ms. Alvarez. Sorry.
Let's get this cleaned up.
Appreciate you. Seriously.
lines.action: finger-guns at the player once Alvarez's back is turned, relieved

lines.text: Huh.
Later, in the hallway, Maya passes by and doesn't quite meet Ellis's eyes. It's a small thing. It doesn't feel small.
Jordan catches up to Ellis at the lockers, easy smile back in place, like nothing happened.
Hey — hey, so. Let's just keep this simple, get through Friday, yeah? No need to make it a whole thing, Captain.

lines.action: leans against the lockers a few feet away, arms crossed, watching

lines.text: Okay but— what exactly are we keeping simple.
You know. All of it. The terrarium, the— whatever happened with Maya. It's not that deep. We present, we're done, everybody moves on.
Moves on to what.
Man, why do you always— I'm just saying. It's Thursday. Presentation's tomorrow. Can we not.
Ellis finds Ms. Alvarez still grading at her desk after the last bell.
Ms. Alvarez? Can I talk to you about the terrarium project. Actually.
I'm listening.
lines.action: sets down her pen, taps it twice

lines.text: It's about... what really happened. With the terrarium and Maya and—
lines.action: trails off, loses her nerve for a second

lines.text: I'm not asking you to snitch. I'm asking you to tell me what you saw. There's a difference, and you know it.
The terrarium isn't ours. Jordan borrowed it from his sister's old project, we just relabeled it. And when it broke — Jordan said Maya dropped it, but he did. His elbow hit it.
Thank you for telling me. That took something, coming in here.
I should've said it sooner.
You said it before Friday. That's what matters right now.
You get an extension. Not because the mess goes away — it happened — but because you told me the truth about it, and that's worth something in this room. I'll talk to Jordan myself.
Jordan is called in. He doesn't look at Ellis on his way past. Maya, waiting by the door, catches Ellis's eye instead — something in her face has shifted.
You actually did it.
Yeah.
Okay. Okay, good. I'll redo the labels tonight. For real this time.
Okay but— can we just. Talk. For real, before tomorrow.
Talk about what.
About the terrarium not being ours. About what you said about Maya.
...It's fine, it's handled—
lines.action: goes very still, deciding whether to be brave or easy

lines.text: It's not handled. It's just quiet.
You said I dropped it. In front of Ms. Alvarez. I didn't say anything because I didn't know what to—
...Yeah. Okay. My bad. I'll— yeah. I'll fix it with her.
And the labels. We should redo the ones that aren't real. Tonight, if we have to.
Fine. FINE! We'll redo it. Can everybody stop looking at me like that.
Nobody's looking at you like anything.
Everybody's looking at me like that.
Thank you. For saying it.
It's not fixed all at once. But for the first time all week, it feels like they're actually building the same thing.
Yeah, okay. Let's just get through tomorrow.
See, that's what I'm talking about. Knew you'd get it.
lines.action: grins, claps the player on the shoulder, relieved

lines.text: Down the hall, Maya closes her locker a little too hard. She doesn't look over.
Huh.
lines.action: passing by, doesn't stop, keeps walking

lines.text: It's just one presentation. It'll be fine.
The terrarium sits on the table in front of the class. Ms. Alvarez watches the Q&A with her usual unreadable calm.
Good model. Now — walk me through the actual process. Who did what, and what was hardest about it?
Maya did most of the research — the pH balance, the plant compatibility, all of it. Honestly the hardest part was just... trusting the process instead of rushing it.
I— yeah. I mean, there were a few nights of a lot of double-checking. But it was worth it.
I built the frame. Twice. First one wasn't level.
...And I did labels. Eventually.
Sounds like a real project.
lines.action: small, dry almost-smile, moves to the next group

lines.text: Thank you. For that.
lines.action: beaming, quiet, just to the player

lines.text: Yeah, so we split it up pretty evenly, everybody pulled their weight, it was basically a team effort start to finish—
Anything to add?
No, that's— that's about right.
Alright. Thank you.
lines.action: pen tap, moves on, nothing more
sideways glance at the player, says nothing — it lands anyway

lines.text: Jordan grins like they stuck the landing. Ellis isn't so sure.
The front steps of Ridgemont are mostly empty by the time Ellis heads out. Deshawn falls into step beside her without asking.
So what do you actually think? Not what you think you're supposed to think.
lines.action: hands in pockets, looking straight ahead

lines.text: Take your time. I'm not going anywhere.
lines.action: taps a pencil against his knuckles when she doesn't answer right away

options.text: Fine, let's just use it. It'll save us so much time.
Wait— what if we actually built our own? Split up the real work?

a.action: doesn't argue, just picks up a marker, eyes down

b.action: short laugh, not really a laugh

options.text: That's not what happened. Jordan, you were holding it.
(Stay quiet for now — find Maya later, privately.)
Yeah— that's what happened, Ms. Alvarez.

a.text: That's not what happened. Jordan, you were holding it — your elbow hit it.

b.action: glances at Maya, says nothing yet

options.text: I need to tell Ms. Alvarez what actually happened.
Let's just talk about this. All of us. Right now.
Yeah. Let's just get through tomorrow.

a.text: I need to tell Ms. Alvarez what actually happened. Before tomorrow.

b.text: No. We're going to fix this ourselves. Tonight.

c.text: Yeah. Let's just get through tomorrow.

options.text: Actually, I want to say something about that.
(Let Jordan handle the answer — stay quiet.)

a.text: Actually — I want to say something about that.

b.action: stays quiet, lets Jordan take it

options.text: Doing the right thing was hard. But I'm glad I did it.
Honestly? I'm still figuring out how to handle stuff like that.
It wasn't that big a deal, really.

a.text: It was hard. Doing the right thing, I mean. But I'm glad I did it.

b.text: Honestly? I'm still figuring out how to handle stuff like that.
Fair. Most people never even get that far.

c.text: It wasn't that big a deal, really.

lines.text: You told Alvarez straight up, before you had to. That's not nothing, Ellis. Maya knows it too.
Felt like the floor was gonna open up the whole walk to her desk.
Yeah. That's usually how it feels right before it's the right call.
Jordan still isn't really talking to me.
He will. Or he won't. Either way, you didn't need him to like you to do it.
You backed him up. Twice.
...I know.
Maya noticed both times. So did I.
It wasn't that deep, Deshawn—
...You sound like Jordan now.
You didn't go to Alvarez. You just... made everybody actually talk. That's harder than it looks.
Felt like it took forever. And Jordan sulked the whole time.
Maya's talking to you again like normal. That's not an accident.

lines.action: small smile, doesn't say anything for a second

lines.text: You let it ride. All week.
I know.
Maya still won't look at Jordan straight. You notice that?
...Yeah.
Just checking you're not gonna pretend you didn't.

voiceNotes: Ellis (player): her 'Okay but—' opener is a courage-gathering ritual, not confidence — use it when she's about to disagree, then let her drop it entirely in high-stakes moments (scene-5a, scene-7a) to signal she's stopped hedging. Her nodding-along habit should appear as an action tag right before choices where she's tempted to go along with the group, so the player *feels* the pull toward conflict-avoidance in the staging, not just the text. Jordan: fast and charming until cornered — his lines should get shorter and lose punctuation/jokes the moment he's actually held accountable (scene-7b, scene-5a). Never let him say a full 'I'm sorry' — always the sideways 'my bad, anyway—' reframe. Maya: precise vocabulary is armor; let it crack into short, plain sentences exactly when she's most hurt ('You didn't say anything either.') — the formality should vanish in her rawest lines. Deshawn: economy of language is the point — every line should be able to be read in under two seconds. His silence (empty text, action-only lines) should carry as much weight as his dialogue. Ms. Alvarez never raises her voice or accuses; she reflects questions back ('Walk me through it') and rewards honesty explicitly rather than punishing dishonesty implicitly.

alternatives: It wasn't me — Maya had it!
Maya's the one who was holding it!
alternativeLines.reasoning: Keeping the exact profile quote ('Maya was holding it — she dropped it!') preserves the blurted, panicked cadence specified in character voice examples; alternates offered in case pacing needs a shorter beat.

alternatives: You didn't say it either. Just so you know.
alternativeLines.reasoning: Original line matches the profile's exact quiet, tired, non-accusing tone; alternates are slightly more clipped if a harsher read is wanted in performance.

alternatives: Let's just keep this simple, get through Friday, yeah?
Hey — can we just, like, not make this a whole thing before Friday?
alternativeLines.reasoning: Removing the invented nickname 'Captain' softens the line if it reads as too jokey for the tension of the scene.
